title: pultenaea ternata
intro: Pultenaea ternata, also known as bush pea, is a low-growing, spreading shrub native to eastern Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bush pea has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be used externally to treat skin conditions and wounds, and internally to support overall health and well-being.
